raw_themes.name: Context-over-filename retrieval
raw_themes.short_desc: Make files findable by surrounding metadata/links, not filename or folder.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: finding files by how you think about them, not by what they're named

raw_themes.name: Manual metadata doesn't scale
raw_themes.short_desc: Hand-authored metadata as primary retrieval breaks past ~200 items.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Works for 20 files, breaks at 200. Automate metadata extraction instead.

raw_themes.name: Capture why-at-ingestion
raw_themes.short_desc: Record why a source matters at ingestion time, not just what it is.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: writing _why_ a file matters at the point of ingestion is something our handler skills could do better

raw_candidates.name: Context-over-filename retrieval principle
raw_candidates.description: Make files findable by surrounding metadata and linked context rather than filename or folder path.

raw_candidates.name: Subfolder-per-project attachment pattern
raw_candidates.description: Auto-create attachments/ subfolder per project folder. Keeps assets co-located.

youtube-qinuqwl4e-k.relevance_to_home: Low direct relevance. SoNash's CAS already implements richer retrieval (SQLite FTS + structured tags + extraction-journal) than this Obsidian UI tutorial. The one transferable idea is capturing 'why analyzed' at ingestion time in analysis.json.

raw_themes.name: Caption-first, Whisper-fallback media extraction
raw_themes.short_desc: Check captions before running transcription; most YouTube videos already have them.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Both bedrock and this repo skip captions and go straight to transcription. pytubefix has yt.captions

raw_themes.name: faster-whisper over openai-whisper for batch
raw_themes.short_desc: CTranslate2 impl is 4x faster with lower memory; correct choice for batch local transcription.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: `faster_whisper` over `openai-whisper`: The author chose the CTranslate2 implementation, which is 4x faster with lower memory

raw_themes.name: Async download + sync GPU transcription
raw_themes.short_desc: Parallelize network I/O with asyncio semaphore; serialize GPU compute.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Downloads use asyncio with a semaphore (max 4 concurrent), but transcription runs sequentially per video. This is correct

raw_themes.name: Tuned Whisper params beat defaults
raw_themes.short_desc: beam_size=10 + vad_filter=True prevents hallucination-on-silence; operational knowledge not in docs.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True to skip silence and prevent Whisper hallucination-on-silence

raw_themes.name: Modular handlers over monolithic scripts
raw_themes.short_desc: Single-file tools work for personal use but block composition; T27 pipeline needs modular handlers.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Don't build monolithic single-file tools. This repo puts everything — config, download, transcription, NLP, output — in one 280-line file

raw_themes.name: Cost-as-first-class for cloud-vs-local decisions
raw_themes.short_desc: Explicit $/min estimation makes local-vs-API tradeoff concrete.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: OpenAI Whisper API: $0.006/min, less accurate. Local faster-whisper: free, more accurate, requires GPU. 1-hour video = $0.36 API vs $0 local.

raw_themes.name: Hard-coded config anti-pattern
raw_themes.short_desc: Module-level vars including API keys; no CLI/env/config layer.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Module-level variables like `convert_single_video = 1` and `openai_api_key = 'REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_API_KEY'` are the opposite of twelve-factor app configuration

raw_candidates.name: faster-whisper local transcription pipeline
raw_candidates.description: Complete local Whisper lifecycle: CUDA detection, large-v3 model, beam_size=10, vad_filter=True, segment iteration with tqdm, metadata capture. ~50 lines covering all operational edge cases.

raw_candidates.name: Whisper tuned params (beam_size=10, vad_filter)
raw_candidates.description: beam_size=10 (vs default 5) for accuracy. vad_filter=True to skip silence and prevent Whisper hallucination-on-silence issue.

raw_candidates.name: Caption-first + Whisper-fallback architecture
raw_candidates.description: Cross-repo insight: check captions first (pytubefix yt.captions), only fallback to Whisper for the ~20% without captions. Dramatically cheaper and faster.

raw_candidates.name: Async download + sync GPU transcription pattern
raw_candidates.description: asyncio.Semaphore(4) for concurrent downloads, sequential GPU transcription. Network I/O parallelizes, GPU compute doesn't.

raw_candidates.name: faster-whisper library reference
raw_candidates.description: CTranslate2 Whisper implementation. 4x faster than OpenAI whisper. The local transcription engine candidate for T27.

raw_candidates.name: CUDA/GPU detection + CPU fallback pattern
raw_candidates.description: numba.cuda.is_available() check, Anaconda toolkit path detection, float16 vs auto compute type selection.

raw_candidates.name: SpaCy + compromise.js two-stage NLP
raw_candidates.description: Server-side SpaCy for sentence splitting during transcription, client-side compromise.js for paragraph restructuring in reader.

raw_candidates.name: API vs local cost tradeoff ($0.006/min)
raw_candidates.description: Concrete $/min data for Whisper API vs local. 1-hour video = $0.36 API vs $0 local (but GPU required).

raw_candidates.name: compromise.js client-side NLP
raw_candidates.description: 75KB client-side NLP library. Sentence/paragraph detection in browser. Potential for SoNash frontend text processing.

raw_candidates.name: Monolithic single-file tool anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: 280 lines, no CLI, no config file, hard-coded vars. Works for personal use, impossible to integrate.

raw_candidates.name: Skip-captions-always anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: Both bedrock and this repo transcribe every video from scratch despite pytubefix having caption extraction.

raw_candidates.name: Hard-coded source config anti-pattern
raw_candidates.description: Module-level variables for config including API keys. No CLI args, no env vars. Anti-twelve-factor.

bulk-transcribe-youtube-playlist.relevance_to_home: High relevance to T27 (Media Extraction Deep-Research) and JASON-OS portable-tool vision. Provides the local-first Whisper reference implementation, plus the caption-first+fallback insight that reframes the entire media-extraction pipeline architecture for /media-analysis.

raw_themes.name: Engine-fallback as the extraction product
raw_themes.short_desc: Pluggable extraction engines with named NoEnginesLeftError and documented retry order.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: eight engines (fetch, fire-engine, playwright, pdf, document, wikipedia, index, utils) coordinated by a fallback-list builder and a retry tracker

raw_themes.name: LLM-ready markdown as first-class output
raw_themes.short_desc: Convert sources straight to markdown rather than plaintext-then-remarkdown.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: the old code 'converted to just plaintext' and the new code 'converts straight to markdown'

raw_themes.name: Capability-gated testing (not CI-fenced)
raw_themes.short_desc: Gate tests by runtime capability env vars so same file runs in all envs.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Every test file can run in every environment; the gate decides whether assertions actually execute

raw_themes.name: Examples-as-documentation
raw_themes.short_desc: 58 self-contained example apps as product-marketing-in-code; each pairs framework+LLM.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 58 example apps are a product-marketing-in-code pattern worth stealing

raw_themes.name: Minimal CLAUDE.md beats verbose
raw_themes.short_desc: 19-line CLAUDE.md with capability-gating + harness usage outperforms 200-line variants.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 19-line CLAUDE.md that beats most 200-line variants

raw_themes.name: Docs-absence as anti-pattern
raw_themes.short_desc: 1162 files + one mermaid diagram + no docs/ concentrates knowledge in founding engineers.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 1162 files and one mermaid diagram. A new contributor learns the architecture by reading controllers, services, and CLAUDE.md

raw_themes.name: Polyglot SDK maintenance tax
raw_themes.short_desc: Seven hand-rolled SDKs enforce parity via OpenAPI+test-suite rather than codegen.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Seven SDKs (JS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, Elixir, + a TS playwright service) re-implement the wire contract each time

raw_candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback chain
raw_candidates.description: Pluggable extraction engines coordinated by a fallback-list builder. buildFallbackList → scrapeURLWithEngine → try next or NoEnginesLeftError. Named failure mode, documented order, engine-level telemetry.

raw_candidates.name: Capability-gated test pattern
raw_candidates.description: Gate tests by runtime capability (env vars) not by CI fence. Same test file runs in all environments; gate decides whether assertions execute.

raw_candidates.name: Harness-as-boot-orchestrator
raw_candidates.description: Single harness.ts script owns service boot order (API + workers + emulator) before test runner. CLAUDE.md mandates it; no ad-hoc pnpm start.

raw_candidates.name: Version-parallel controller trees
raw_candidates.description: Parallel v0/v1/v2 controller directories with their own test fixtures. Three OpenAPI specs frozen at repo root.

raw_candidates.name: Redlock distributed cache serialization
raw_candidates.description: redlock.using([key], ttlMs, ...) to serialise cache updates across multiple pods. Prevents race conditions on shared cache slots.

raw_candidates.name: Multi-agent orchestration via openai_swarm
raw_candidates.description: Reference implementation of multi-agent coordination around a single scraping task. Worth reading to inform SoNash agent teams and JASON-OS agent boundary design.

raw_candidates.name: Deep Research API usage pattern
raw_candidates.description: How firecrawl's Deep Research API is invoked in practice (apartment-finder). Interactive preference gathering → research call → LLM analysis → structured JSON.

raw_candidates.name: GitHub repo analysis via firecrawl Map API
raw_candidates.description: examples/gemini-github-analyzer uses firecrawl's Map API to walk a GitHub repo. Direct domain overlap with SoNash /repo-analysis.

raw_candidates.name: Docs-to-OpenAPI extraction
raw_candidates.description: examples/turning_docs_into_api_specs — convert a documentation site into an OpenAPI spec via scraping. Informs SoNash document-analysis handler.

raw_candidates.name: Commercial-OSS feature tiering via .env
raw_candidates.description: Fire-engine is hosted-only; self-hosted users get documented limited CAPTCHA handling. Pattern: tier features by backing service availability, document the split.

raw_candidates.name: scrapeURL engine-fallback README
raw_candidates.description: Mermaid signal flow diagram for the engine fallback chain. The whole extraction thesis in four nodes.

raw_candidates.name: 58-example cookbook pattern
raw_candidates.description: The examples/ directory as product-marketing-in-code. 58 self-contained apps, each pairing firecrawl with a specific LLM or framework.

raw_candidates.name: CLAUDE.md minimal-actionable exemplar
raw_candidates.description: 19-line CLAUDE.md that beats most 200-line variants. Test-first, E2E preferred, capability-gating, harness usage.

raw_candidates.name: Zero architecture prose docs
raw_candidates.description: 1162 files, one mermaid diagram, no docs/ directory. New contributors pay the ramp-up tax. File as evidence in 'do we need this ADR?' conversations.

raw_candidates.name: Two queue systems coexisting without migration plan
raw_candidates.description: BullMQ and nuq-postgres both active in services/. Rationale undocumented. Suggests in-progress migration with no exit criteria.

raw_candidates.name: Hand-rolled polyglot SDK parity
raw_candidates.description: Seven SDKs re-implement the wire contract manually. Adding an endpoint is a 7-file change. Prefer codegen-from-OpenAPI.

raw_candidates.name: Hidden feature flags via env vars
raw_candidates.description: Branches behaviour through SELF_HOST.md env vars with no documented feature-flag layer. Breaks down as team grows.

firecrawl.relevance_to_home: High relevance to SoNash's website-analysis + document-analysis handlers and JASON-OS testing infrastructure. The engine-fallback pattern maps directly onto expanding SoNash website-analysis beyond superpowers-chrome; capability-gated testing is a direct win for Firebase Functions integration tests; the 58-example cookbook pattern and minimal CLAUDE.md are re-usable templates.

raw_themes.name: Plugin registry via side-effect registration
raw_themes.short_desc: Single-method extractor interface + init() auto-registration + blank imports + universal fallback.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: a new site extractor is 4 steps: create file, implement interface, call Register in init(), add blank import

raw_themes.name: Per-site CI isolation for arms-race code
raw_themes.short_desc: Each extractor gets its own workflow + badge; independent failure signals.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: When YouTube breaks (and it will), the badge goes red and only youtube needs fixing

raw_themes.name: Meta-CI: generate workflows from template
raw_themes.short_desc: Node script programmatically generates 45 GitHub Actions workflows from one template.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: a Node script that generates 45 GitHub Actions workflows from a single template

raw_themes.name: Security-as-afterthought in popular tools
raw_themes.short_desc: InsecureSkipVerify+hardcoded credentials despite 46-extractor scale and 26-platform distribution.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: TLS certificate verification is globally disabled (InsecureSkipVerify: true)

raw_themes.name: Documentation absent for contributors
raw_themes.short_desc: 46 extractors, 30 contributors, 23-line CONTRIBUTING.md; code grew without contributor onboarding.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 46 extractors, 30 contributors, 23-line CONTRIBUTING.md, zero architecture docs

raw_candidates.name: init()-based plugin registry with domain routing
raw_candidates.description: Single-method Extractor interface + global map + init() auto-registration + blank imports + universal fallback. 46 extractors, zero boilerplate. Go-specific but principle universal.

raw_candidates.name: CI workflow generation from template
raw_candidates.description: Node script generates 45 GitHub Actions workflows from a single template. Per-extractor CI isolation. Meta-CI.

raw_candidates.name: Per-site CI isolation pattern
raw_candidates.description: Each of 46 extractors has own CI workflow and README badge. Independent failure signals per target.

raw_candidates.name: HARDCODED_CREDENTIALS_IN_SOURCE
raw_candidates.description: Twitter bearer token and Weibo session cookie checked into git. Normalizes credential exposure.

raw_candidates.name: DISABLED_TLS_VERIFICATION
raw_candidates.description: InsecureSkipVerify: true on all HTTPS connections. Global MITM vulnerability.

raw_candidates.name: DOCUMENTATION_ABSENT_FOR_CONTRIBUTORS
raw_candidates.description: 46 extractors, 30 contributors, 23-line CONTRIBUTING.md, zero architecture docs. Growth without onboarding = bus-factor risk.

lux-video-downloader.relevance_to_home: Medium relevance. The CI workflow generation + per-target CI isolation patterns inform JASON-OS CI/CD design, and the plugin registry is an architecture reference point for SoNash skill routing (which uses directory-listing discovery). The security anti-patterns confirm SoNash's existing guardrails (no direct secrets, TLS verification enforced).

raw_themes.name: Claude Code plugin packaging as shippable unit
raw_themes.short_desc: marketplace.json bundles MCP server + skills + metadata into installable plugin.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: .claude-plugin/marketplace.json bundles MCP server + skills into a single installable unit

raw_themes.name: Frontmatter-level skill invocation gates
raw_themes.short_desc: allowed-tools and disable-model-invocation enforce behavior via manifest, not prose.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: allowed-tools: Bash(qmd:*), mcp__qmd__* ... disable-model-invocation: true to prevent accidental auto-triggers on risky operations

raw_themes.name: MCP server as CLI subcommand with daemon mode
raw_themes.short_desc: Three transports (stdio/http/daemon) let one tool serve multiple MCP client models.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: qmd ships its MCP server as a CLI subcommand (qmd mcp). Three transports shown: Stdio (default, per-client subprocess) HTTP foreground HTTP daemon with PID file management

raw_themes.name: Hybrid retrieval: BM25 + vector + LLM rerank + query expansion
raw_themes.short_desc: Formal fusion algorithm (RRF + position-aware blending) beats ad-hoc flag filtering.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: it knows that a knowledge base search engine needs BM25 AND vector search AND reranking — not one of them

raw_themes.name: Formal query DSL with intent disambiguation
raw_themes.short_desc: EBNF grammar with typed sub-queries (lex:, vec:, hyde:) and intent: line.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: it's a formal EBNF grammar with typed sub-queries (`lex:`, `vec:`, `hyde:`), an `intent:` disambiguation line, and parsing constraints

raw_themes.name: Structure-aware markdown chunking
raw_themes.short_desc: Scored break points (H1=100, code fence=80, paragraph=20) + squared distance decay.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Scored break points + AST for code ... qmd respects structure

raw_themes.name: Fixture-based eval harness for skills
raw_themes.short_desc: Difficulty-tiered queries + precision@k/recall/MRR/F1 across 4 backends.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Test queries categorized easy/medium/hard with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends

raw_themes.name: Privacy-first local inference via node-llama-cpp
raw_themes.short_desc: GGUF + createRankingContext() lets a Node/TS app do cross-encoder reranking without API keys.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: node-llama-cpp for privacy-first local inference ... SoNash's privacy-first vision could use node-llama-cpp for local AI without API keys

raw_candidates.name: Claude Code plugin manifest (marketplace.json)
raw_candidates.description: Plugin manifest bundling MCP server + skills + metadata for 'claude plugin marketplace add' installation. Single source of truth for Claude Code plugin discovery.

raw_candidates.name: Skill frontmatter: allowed-tools + disable-model-invocation
raw_candidates.description: Frontmatter declarations that restrict tool access and prevent auto-invocation. Enforces behavior via manifest, not prose.

raw_candidates.name: MCP server as CLI subcommand (3 transports)
raw_candidates.description: MCP server accessed via 'qmd mcp' subcommand. Stdio + HTTP foreground + HTTP daemon with PID file. Daemon avoids expensive model reload.

raw_candidates.name: Query DSL with typed sub-queries (EBNF grammar)
raw_candidates.description: Formal EBNF grammar for search queries. Typed sub-queries (lex/vec/hyde), optional intent disambiguation, parsing constraints.

raw_candidates.name: Scored markdown chunking algorithm
raw_candidates.description: Break point scoring (H1=100, H2=90, code fence=80, paragraph=20, line=1) with squared distance decay. 900 tokens/chunk + 15% overlap.

raw_candidates.name: RRF + position-aware blending
raw_candidates.description: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (k=60) with original-query 2x weight, top-rank bonus, then position-aware blend after rerank (75/60/40% RRF weight for ranks 1-3/4-10/11+).

raw_candidates.name: Fixture-based eval harness with difficulty tiers
raw_candidates.description: Test queries categorized easy/medium/hard with expected docs. Precision@k, recall, MRR, F1 across 4 backends. 'qmd bench' CLI for reproducible quality measurement.

raw_candidates.name: Release skill with disabled auto-invocation
raw_candidates.description: Release workflow: gather context via script, commit outstanding, write changelog, cut release, watch CI, push on confirmation. Enforces exact dep pinning. disable-model-invocation: true.

raw_candidates.name: node-llama-cpp for privacy-first local inference
raw_candidates.description: Node.js wrapper for llama.cpp. Runs GGUF models locally with Metal/CUDA/Vulkan. createRankingContext() + rankAndSort() for cross-encoder reranking.

raw_candidates.name: AST-aware code chunking via tree-sitter
raw_candidates.description: tree-sitter parsing for TS/JS/Python/Go/Rust. Chunks at function/class/import boundaries. Optional via --chunk-strategy auto.

raw_candidates.name: Daemon mode with PID file management
raw_candidates.description: HTTP server with --daemon flag. PID at ~/.cache/qmd/mcp.pid. 'qmd mcp stop' reads PID and terminates. Idle context disposal after 5 min.

raw_candidates.name: LoRA SFT on small LLMs for domain tasks
raw_candidates.description: LoRA rank 16, alpha 32. ~2290 examples, 5 epochs, 2e-4 LR cosine. Qwen3-1.7B base. 92% eval score. $1.50 per run.

raw_candidates.name: HuggingFace Jobs for cheap cloud training
raw_candidates.description: hf jobs uv run --flavor a10g-large executes SFT in cloud for ~$1.50. Self-contained scripts.

raw_candidates.name: Rule-based reward function for RL
raw_candidates.description: 5-dimension scoring (Format 30, Diversity 30, HyDE 20, Quality 20, Entity -45 to +20). No LLM judge — deterministic and fast.

raw_candidates.name: Nix flake with home-manager module
raw_candidates.description: flake.nix with homeModules.default providing programs.qmd.enable option. Reproducible install.

raw_candidates.name: Fine-tuning before exhausting prompt engineering
raw_candidates.description: Fine-tuning is expensive and inflexible. qmd tried GEPA prompt optimization first before SFT. Exhaust prompting before fine-tuning.

raw_candidates.name: Single-runtime CI when cross-runtime is claimed
raw_candidates.description: If you claim cross-runtime support (Node+Bun) without matrix testing, you're shipping bugs.

raw_candidates.name: Caret ranges in reproducible-install packages
raw_candidates.description: qmd pins all deps to exact versions — critical for tools shipping platform-specific binaries. Caret ranges break transitive deps.

raw_candidates.name: Skill without invocation gate on destructive ops
raw_candidates.description: Release/deploy/destructive skills that Claude can auto-invoke create risk. Should declare disable-model-invocation.

qmd.relevance_to_home: Extremely high relevance — arguably the most JASON-OS-relevant source in the corpus. qmd is a near-ideal reference for how Claude Code plugins should be packaged (marketplace.json), how skill invocation should be gated (disable-model-invocation), and how MCP should ship as a CLI subcommand. The retrieval stack (hybrid BM25+vector+rerank, EBNF query DSL, scored chunking, eval harness) is directly applicable to SoNash's recall/synthesize skills over the 277-candidate extraction-journal.

raw_themes.name: Benchmark registry as intellectual honesty
raw_themes.short_desc: 7 methods compared via 2 independent scorers on published dataset; anyone can add a converter.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Seven methods compared (marker, llamaparse, mathpix, docling, olmocr, mistral, ground-truth) with two independent scoring approaches

raw_themes.name: 3-stage pipeline (provider/processor/renderer)
raw_themes.short_desc: Clean separation with 6 documented extension points lets users swap any stage.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: extract with Surya models, transform with 25+ processors, render to Markdown/JSON/HTML

raw_themes.name: DI via constructor introspection
raw_themes.short_desc: resolve_dependencies() inspects __init__ signatures — no registration ceremony.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: The dependency injection via resolve_dependencies() (which inspects **init** signatures) is clever — no registration ceremony, just match constructor parameters

raw_themes.name: Security-as-afterthought for untrusted input
raw_themes.short_desc: Shell injection + path traversal + credential exposure in a PDF processing tool.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Shell injection in chunk_convert.py (shell=True with user input), path traversal in file upload, AWS credentials as CLI defaults

raw_themes.name: GPL license blocks adoption
raw_themes.short_desc: GPL-3.0 means any derivative must be GPL; extract patterns only, never import code.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: GPL-3.0 copyleft blocks adoption as dependency. Must extract patterns only, never import code.

raw_themes.name: Contributor-hostile governance despite star count
raw_themes.short_desc: 33.7K stars + 403 issues + 27 contributors + no CONTRIBUTING/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: 33.7K stars, 403 open issues, 27 contributors, no CONTRIBUTING.md, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT. Optimized for users, not for building a contributor community

raw_candidates.name: Benchmark registry (7 methods, 2 scorers)
raw_candidates.description: Pluggable registry for comparing implementations against same dataset with independent scoring. METHOD_REGISTRY + SCORE_REGISTRY + published dataset.

raw_candidates.name: 3-stage pipeline (provider/processor/renderer)
raw_candidates.description: Clean separation: extract → transform → render. Each stage independently extensible with 6 extension points.

raw_candidates.name: ConfigParser introspection for component discovery
raw_candidates.description: Auto-discovers available builders, processors, converters by inspecting module structure.

raw_candidates.name: Session-scoped test fixtures for ML models
raw_candidates.description: Load expensive models once per session. Custom pytest markers for parameterization.

raw_candidates.name: GPU-enforced CI as quality gate
raw_candidates.description: Tests run on t4_gpu runners. Expensive = strict enforcement of production-like testing.

raw_candidates.name: GPL_LICENSE_TRAP
raw_candidates.description: GPL-3.0 copyleft blocks adoption as dependency. Must extract patterns only, never import code.

raw_candidates.name: SECURITY_AS_AFTERTHOUGHT
raw_candidates.description: Shell injection, path traversal, credential exposure in a tool processing untrusted PDFs.

raw_candidates.name: CONTRIBUTOR_HOSTILE_GOVERNANCE
raw_candidates.description: 33.7K stars, 403 issues, no CONTRIBUTING, no CODE_OF_CONDUCT. Users optimized, contributors not.

vikparuchuri-marker.relevance_to_home: Medium-high relevance. The benchmark registry pattern is directly applicable to T28 skill quality measurement (compare repo-analysis v4.3 vs v4.5 against a fixture set). The 3-stage pipeline architecture is an architecture reference for decomposing /analyze into composable stages. Security + governance anti-patterns reinforce SoNash's existing guardrails.

raw_themes.name: Meta-tool pattern for dynamic discovery
raw_themes.short_desc: Agent uses 6 meta-tools (search/get-schema/execute/…) to discover tools at runtime, not load all 1000+.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Composio provides 6 meta tools — search, get-schema, auth, execute, workbench, bash. The agent discovers tools at runtime and loads only what's needed

raw_themes.name: Native vs MCP tradeoff with token numbers
raw_themes.short_desc: 5-server MCP setup costs ~55K tokens pre-conversation; native gives control+interception.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: a 5-server MCP setup consumes ~55K tokens before conversation starts

raw_themes.name: llms.txt as AI-readable docs standard
raw_themes.short_desc: Publish /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt indexes so other LLM systems can consume your docs.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Composio publishes AI-readable site indexes at `/llms.txt` and `/llms-full.txt`

raw_themes.name: Event trigger dual model (webhook+polling)
raw_themes.short_desc: Use webhooks where supported, polling elsewhere; makes agents event-driven rather than invocation-driven.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Webhook for real-time capable services, polling for those without. User-scoped, account-bound.

raw_themes.name: Integration tax as a platform business
raw_themes.short_desc: OAuth flows + token refresh + scoping + multi-tenant is a massive surface every agent builder hits.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Composio understands the integration tax — the cost of connecting AI agents to real services

raw_candidates.name: Meta-tool pattern for dynamic tool discovery
raw_candidates.description: Instead of loading all tool definitions, provide a small set of meta tools (search, get-schema, execute). Agent discovers at runtime. Solves context window bloat. Applicable to JASON-OS at 100+ skills.

raw_candidates.name: Native vs MCP tradeoff framework with token cost data
raw_candidates.description: Native: context control + interception. MCP: simplicity + client-agnostic. 55K token overhead for 5 MCP servers. Decision framework for when to use which.

raw_candidates.name: llms.txt standard for AI-readable documentation
raw_candidates.description: Standardized llms.txt and llms-full.txt at site root for LLM consumption. Makes docs AI-discoverable.

raw_candidates.name: Event trigger dual model (webhook + polling) for agent reactivity
raw_candidates.description: Webhook for real-time capable services, polling for those without. User-scoped, account-bound. Pattern for event-driven agents.

docs-composio-dev.relevance_to_home: High relevance for JASON-OS Domain 02a (MCP infrastructure). The meta-tool discovery pattern directly addresses the context-bloat problem of 72+ SoNash skills; the native-vs-MCP framework with concrete 55K-token data is a decision input for how JASON-OS exposes capabilities. The hosted-service dependency conflicts with local-first vision, so patterns transfer, product does not.

raw_themes.name: Exploration-over-conclusion as reasoning principle
raw_themes.short_desc: Force the model not to answer until it has explored exhaustively.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: forcing conclusions kills exploration ... making the LLM's job to _not_ answer until it has explored exhaustively

raw_themes.name: System-prompt thinking superseded by native reasoning
raw_themes.short_desc: Extended thinking is now built into Claude/o-series/Gemini; prompt-level hacks are historical.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: Claude's extended thinking, OpenAI's o-series reasoning models, and Gemini's thinking mode all implement 'think before answering' natively and more effectively than a system prompt can

raw_themes.name: Minimum-exploration threshold before convergence
raw_themes.short_desc: Convergence loops might benefit from a mandatory pre-converge exploration pass.
raw_themes.evidence_quote: convergence loops converge as soon as the tally passes. There might be cases where delaying convergence ... produces better results

raw_candidates.name: Exploration-over-conclusion as skill design principle
raw_candidates.description: Reasoning should explore until conclusions emerge naturally. Validates brainstorm skill Phase 1 philosophy and could inform convergence-loop termination criteria.

raw_candidates.name: Contemplator tag pattern as skill thinking block
raw_candidates.description: <contemplator>/<final_answer> prompt-level structured reasoning. Historical reference — Claude's native <thinking> supersedes.

maharshi-pandya-gist-4aeccbe1.relevance_to_home: Low direct relevance. SoNash's brainstorm + deep-plan + convergence-loop skills already implement structured reasoning more effectively than a single system prompt. The one transferable idea is adding a 'minimum exploration' pre-converge gate before convergence-loop termination.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Media extraction pipeline architecture (captions-first, engine-fallback, pluggable stages)
initial_clusters.short_desc: Multiple sources converge on pluggable/stageable extraction: firecrawl's 8-engine fallback, marker's 3-stage pipeline with 6 extension points, bulk-transcribe's caption-first+whisper-fallback insight, and lux's init()-registered extractor interface. The shared architecture pattern: named stages, documented fallback/error modes, per-target isolation.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Claude Code plugin / skill governance via frontmatter

member_themes.theme_name: Claude Code plugin packaging as shippable unit

initial_clusters.short_desc: qmd and composio converge on the same problem from different angles: at scale (81 SoNash skills, 1000+ Composio toolkits), you need either (a) packaging + frontmatter-declared invocation gates or (b) meta-tool discovery. Both are alternatives to 'load all skill definitions'.

Three independent sources show that sustainable quality gates require formal measurement infrastructure: marker's 7-method/2-scorer registry with published dataset, qmd's difficulty-tiered fixtures with precision@k/recall/MRR, firecrawl's capability-gated tests. SoNash has informal self-audit but no comparative benchmarking.

Both tools at 30K+ stars ship with hardcoded credentials (lux) or shell injection + path traversal + credential exposure (marker). Confirms SoNash CLAUDE.md §5 anti-pattern guardrails (sanitizeError, path-traversal regex, no hardcoded secrets) are in the right place; real-world OSS routinely violates them.

Three popular repos (106K/30K/33K stars) all grew code without growing contributor onboarding: 1162-file firecrawl with one mermaid diagram, lux's 23-line CONTRIBUTING.md, marker's missing CONTRIBUTING/CODE_OF_CONDUCT. Validates SoNash's investment in CLAUDE.md + 72+ SKILL.md + CONVENTIONS.md.

initial_clusters.cluster_name: Cost/tradeoff transparency as signal of engineering maturity

member_themes.theme_name: Native vs MCP tradeoff with token numbers

initial_clusters.short_desc: Two sources stand out by quantifying tradeoffs with concrete numbers: bulk-transcribe's $0.006/min API vs $0 local, composio's 55K-token overhead for 5 MCP servers. Pattern: publish real numbers for local-vs-cloud and native-vs-MCP decisions. JASON-OS should measure its own MCP overhead.

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is Sober Nashville — a recovery notebook for the sobriety community. One would expect at least some sources on trauma-informed UX, harm-reduction content patterns, or community moderation.
absence_signals.suggestion: Slice has zero recovery-domain sources. If synthesis surfaces this gap overall, queue a targeted /website-analysis of recovery-focused products (Sober Grid, InTheRooms, I Am Sober app).

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash is Firebase-native (Firestore + Auth + App Check + Functions). Expected to see Firebase-specific patterns for App Check, security rules, or Cloud Functions architecture.
absence_signals.suggestion: None of the 8 sources in this slice use Firebase. For SoNash-direct applicability, corpus needs Firebase-native references — e.g., a Next.js+Firebase SaaS starter or Firebase Extensions patterns.

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash handles journal entries + daily logs + recovery data — the privacy-first posture is core. Expected guidance on client-side encryption, zero-knowledge storage, or PII handling.
absence_signals.suggestion: qmd approaches privacy-first via local inference (node-llama-cpp) but doesn't address cloud-persisted personal data. Consider sourcing an analyzed reference on Signal-protocol-style client-side encryption or PII redaction libraries.

absence_signals.expected_because: SoNash has 14 pre-commit checks + TDMS tech-debt pipeline + health checkers. Expected to find cross-source validation of these patterns.
absence_signals.suggestion: Firecrawl's CLAUDE.md capability-gating is close but narrower. No source in this slice covers a full pre-commit governance pipeline comparable to SoNash's. A targeted source search for 'repo-level governance as code' would fill this.

absence_signals.expected_because: A recovery notebook that ever adds social features will need content moderation tuned for sensitive populations.
absence_signals.suggestion: Zero slice coverage. Defer until SoNash actually plans social features.

notes: youtube-qinuqwl4e-k is a deliberately low-signal source (creator-view classifies it 'park-for-later' with Applicability=Not transferable). Including its 2 candidates for completeness; parent should likely deprioritize in merged ranking.

maharshi-pandya-gist-4aeccbe1 is also a low-signal source (score Needs Work 45). The creator-view explicitly states its technique is superseded by native extended-thinking. Surfacing for completeness; most value is absence-style (confirms SoNash's structured reasoning is ahead).

qmd stands out as the highest-density JASON-OS-relevant source in the slice with 19 candidates and multiple strong themes across packaging, MCP, retrieval, and eval. Parent may want to weight it heavily in cross-slice clustering.

firecrawl, qmd, and marker all contain cross-source connections referenced in their value-map.json (firecrawl ↔ marker, qmd ↔ firecrawl, qmd ↔ HKUDS/CLI-Anything, marker ↔ MinerU). The engine-fallback ↔ 3-stage-pipeline ↔ plugin-registry connection is the strongest architectural cluster across the slice.

Security/governance anti-patterns appear in 2 of 8 sources (lux, marker) with nearly identical severity. This validates CLAUDE.md §5's explicit anti-pattern guardrails (sanitizeError, path-traversal regex, test mocking). Suggest parent consider whether these merit a 'field-validated anti-patterns' synthesis section.

llms.txt standard (composio) and marketplace.json (qmd) are both discoverability surfaces but at different layers — one for humans-reading-with-AI, one for Claude Code itself. Parent may want to cluster these with any cross-slice discoverability themes.

Cost transparency (bulk-transcribe $0.006/min, composio 55K tokens) is a minor but notable cross-source pattern. JASON-OS currently has no documented MCP token overhead for its 3 servers — potential measurement task.
All 8 sources were readable; no empty or unreadable artifacts encountered.
